Since the tests from issue were not included in the codebase, I've decided to add them in a separate PR.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/21814/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/21814/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/comments/2444027465","html_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/pull/21814#issuecomment-2444027465","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/21814","id":2444027465,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAA3dP86RrOZJ","user":{"login":"V3D3","id":15076943,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE1MDc2OTQz","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/15076943?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/V3D3","html_url":"https://github.com/V3D3","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/V3D3/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/V3D3/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/V3D3/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/V3D3/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/V3D3/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/V3D3/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/V3D3/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/V3D3/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/V3D3/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-10-29T12:06:25Z","updated_at":"2024-10-29T12:06:25Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"Hi, I ended up here trying to find why `np.ma.mean` was not giving the expected `dtype` with my float32 arrays. However, the [`np.mean`/`np.ma.mean` docs](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.mean.html) don't really say that `float32` is to be expected - it just says that the dtype will be used in computation.\r\n\r\nI think if this is merged, it can be made clearer in the docs too, since [Output type determination](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/user/basics.ufuncs.html#ufuncs-output-type) does not explicitly talk about it (as written in the description for the `out` parameter)","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/comments/2444027465/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}
